name: Via Verde de Terra Alta - circular route from Bot
name_original: Vía Verde de Terra Alta – ruta circular desde Bot
region_intro: Touring cycling around Bot primarily features routes along the Via Verde de la Terra Alta, a converted railway line. The landscape is characterized by old railway viaducts and greenways, suggesting a mix of relatively flat sections and gentle gradients. Routes often traverse former rail tunnels and bridges, offering unique perspectives of the surrounding terrain. The region's topography includes moderate elevation changes, providing varied cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Bot The most popular touring cycling route is Via Verde de Terra Alta - circular route from Bot, a 18.2 miles (29.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a comprehensive experience of the region's greenway network. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Viaducto de la Fontcalda – Greenway of Terra Alta loop from Bot, a moderate 11.4 miles (18.3 km) path. This route highlights a prominent viaduct and sections of the greenway. Local touring cyclists also love the Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Old railway viaduct loop from Bot, a 16.2 miles (26.1 km) trail leading through converted railway lines and viaducts, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Bot is defined by converted railway greenways, historic viaducts, and varied terrain with moderate elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Bot are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bot's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Bot?
A: Bot offers a selection of 14 touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. These routes primarily utilize the scenic Via Verde de la Terra Alta, a converted railway line.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Bot?
A: The touring cycling routes around Bot are characterized by converted railway greenways, historic viaducts, and former rail tunnels. You'll encounter a mix of relatively flat sections and gentle gradients, with moderate elevation changes providing a varied cycling experience.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Bot?
A: Yes, Bot features 3 easy touring cycling routes. While specific easy routes are not detailed, the region's greenways offer accessible options for those new to touring cycling or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Bot?
A: Given the nature of the Via Verde de la Terra Alta, many sections are suitable for families. The converted railway lines typically offer smoother, less challenging terrain, making them ideal for cyclists of all ages. Look for routes described as 'easy' or 'moderate' for the best family experience.

Q: What are some of the longer touring cycling routes available in Bot?
A: For a longer ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975282736/via-verde-de-terra-alta-circular-route-from-bot" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de Terra Alta - circular route from Bot&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 18.2 miles (29.2 km). Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bot Station Bar (Railbus) – Via Verde de la Terra Alta loop from Bot&lt;/a&gt;, covering 15 miles (24.1 km).

Q: What unique features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes in Bot are rich with unique features from the old railway line, including historic viaducts and tunnels. You can also explore natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1044274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontcalda&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferrassó Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old railway viaduct&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark on many routes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Bot?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Bot are designed as loops, offering convenient circular journeys. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40355149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viaducto de la Fontcalda – Greenway of Terra Alta loop from Bot&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.4-mile (18.3 km) circular path that highlights a prominent viaduct and sections of the greenway.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Bot?
A: The touring cycling routes in Bot are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique experience of riding along converted railway lines, through tunnels, and over viaducts, as well as the varied terrain.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Bot offers 7 difficult touring cycling routes for those seeking a greater challenge. These routes often include more significant elevation changes and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38232495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Old railway viaduct loop from Bot&lt;/a&gt;, a 16.2-mile (26.1 km) trail with notable elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Bot?
A: While specific seasonal data for Bot is not provided, touring cycling on greenways like the Via Verde de la Terra Alta is generally enjoyable during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be pleasant, but it's always advisable to check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Can I find natural attractions like gorges or rivers along the cycling paths?
A: Absolutely. The region around Bot is home to several natural attractions. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estret del Boter&lt;/a&gt; gorge and follow the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4099363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Canaletes Riverside Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views and refreshing stops along your ride.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by old railway stations or bars?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate elements of the old railway infrastructure, including former station areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38237539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Terra Alta – Bot Station Bar (Railbus) loop from Bot&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.9-mile (11.1 km) route that specifically highlights a former station bar, offering a glimpse into the railway's past.
tips: One of the beautiful bridges that pass over the greenway.

The Sanctuary of the Mare de Déu de la Fontcalda dates from the 14th century, although the current building is from 1753. It is neoclassical in style, with a single nave with a barrel vault and a small transept with an octagonal dome.

This place has been known since the 14th century, where an image of the Mare de Déu de la Fontcalda was venerated. La Font Calda gets its name from the hot springs that gush out at the foot of the sanctuary. The water comes out at 25º and in addition to the sanctuary, buildings were built to house people who came to become priests or simply to spend the weekend or vacation.

Fontcalda, a natural thermal basin inserted in a wonderful gorge. To get there you have to get off the track and go down a steep and short descent. The water temperature is 22 degrees, therefore good for all seasons. It is highly recommended to bring a bathing suit: resisting those waters is difficult.

The Terra Alta Greenway follows the section of the old railway between La Puebla de Híjar (Aragón) and Tortosa. During the route tunnels and viaducts are crossed, as well as wooded landscapes and rural environments.
